output: powershell.exe -Command "& { $rtlo = [char]0x202E; $fileloc = Join-Path ("$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop") ("evil_batch" + $rtlo + "fdp.bat"); Set-Content -Path $fileloc -Value @' @echo off echo Hello World! timeout /t 30 /nobreak exit '@ -Encoding Ascii -Force }"
explanation: This command uses [char]0x syntax to create a Right-to-Left Override (RTLO) character, which is a key element of the Sigma rule. It avoids unnecessary variables and uses a here-string for the payload to maintain readability and test validity.
